_comment: Drives the release page's signed/unsigned rendering. Hand-editable — no build-pipeline change needed. Resolution: overrides['<brandId>/<platform>'] wins, else defaults['<platform>']. When signed:true a store badge+link is shown; when signed:false the per-platform install instructions are shown. {APP} is replaced by the brand's app name (overrides[...].appName, else platform default).

unsigned.headline: Unsigned build — safe, but macOS will ask you to confirm
unsigned.reassurance: This is an independent build that isn't notarized by Apple yet. The app is the same artifact CI produced — verify its SHA-256 if you want certainty. The steps below tell macOS you trust it.
steps: Download the .zip below and double-click to unzip it.
Drag the app into your Applications folder.
macOS may say it "cannot be opened because the developer cannot be verified" — that's expected for an un-notarized build, not a sign of danger.
Open Terminal and run the command below to clear the quarantine flag, then launch the app normally.

unsigned.headline: Unsigned installer — safe, but SmartScreen will warn
unsigned.reassurance: This installer isn't code-signed with an EV certificate yet, so Windows SmartScreen flags new publishers. The build is the same artifact CI produced.
steps: Windows SmartScreen may show "Windows protected your PC" — click "More info".
Click "Run anyway", then follow the installer.

unsigned.headline: Portable build — extract and run
unsigned.reassurance: A self-contained tarball. No installer, no root needed.
steps: Make the binary executable and run it from the extracted folder.
